title: Research: does ! Panic effect track unwrap() calls through iterator chains?
description: Iterator chains encourage .unwrap() as a convenience (.map(|s| s.parse().unwrap())). If Ferrum's ! Panic effect tracks all panic sites including unwrap(), then the function using this chain would have ! Panic in its signature and callers would be warned. If ! Panic is not tracked, unwrap() panics are invisible in the type signature. Need to determine: (1) does the spec say ! Panic is inferred for any function calling unwrap(), (2) does it propagate through closures in iterator chains, (3) are there cases where ! Panic is suppressed or not inferred. If ! Panic is fully tracked, document it prominently. If not, this is a type-safety hole requiring a design decision.
close_reason: Added §2.7 '\! Panic — Sources and Inference': explicit source table (unwrap/expect/panic\!/assert/indexing), non-sources table (arithmetic wrapping, ? operator, checked_add), closure body inference rule, full iterator chain example, design decision note on wrapping arithmetic preserving \! Panic as a meaningful annotation.
